hyperedges.label: Rental Checkout Flow (availability -> rental -> additionals -> payment)
Car Catalog Taxonomy (Car categorized by Brand and Color)

nodes.label: Layered Architecture
nodes.rationale: Project follows layered architecture (API, Business, Data Access, Entity, DTO, Request Models, Core Utilities) for clear separation of concerns.

nodes.label: JWT Authentication
nodes.rationale: Stateless token-based authentication chosen so no server-side session state is kept; client sends 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' and server grants access based on roles. Passwords stored with BCrypt.

nodes.label: Role-Based Access Control
nodes.rationale: User/admin permissions enforced with method-level security (@PreAuthorize); admin-specific endpoints are protected.

nodes.label: Standard API Response Wrapper
nodes.rationale: All API responses use consistent wrapper {success, message, data} so clients can handle success and error uniformly.

nodes.label: Domain-Driven REST API Structure
nodes.rationale: Backend organizes RESTful APIs by business domain (base URL /api/{domain}) so frontend developers can navigate documentation and endpoints per domain; standard HTTP verbs map to CRUD.

nodes.label: Additional Services Pricing Calculation
nodes.rationale: Total cost = service daily price x number of rental days x quantity; calculated by the backend, frontend only displays the breakdown. Each service has maxUnitsPerRental that the UI must enforce.

nodes.label: Brand Entity
nodes.rationale: Brands categorize cars for filtering by manufacturer; names must be unique and at least 3 characters; only admins mutate brands.

nodes.label: Car Entity
nodes.rationale: Main rentable vehicle entity (dailyPrice, modelYear, kilometer, description) referencing Brand and Color; car listing does not filter out currently rented cars, availability must be checked via rental service.

nodes.label: Different City Return Fee
nodes.rationale: When a car is returned to a different city than pickup, a fixed additional fee of 750 currency units is applied; frontend must display this fee when a different return city is selected.

nodes.label: Color Entity
nodes.rationale: Colors categorize cars for filtering; names must be unique, minimum 3 characters; only admins mutate colors.

nodes.label: IndividualCustomer Entity
nodes.rationale: Personal customer with firstName, lastName and unique 11-digit nationalIdentity; public registration allowed.

nodes.label: CorporateCustomer Entity
nodes.rationale: Business customer with companyName and unique 10-digit taxNumber; public registration allowed.

nodes.label: CreditCard Entity
nodes.rationale: Saved customer card; numbers are masked in API responses (first 6 and last 4 digits visible) and CVV is never stored, to limit exposure of card data.

nodes.label: Automatic Payment Amount Calculation
nodes.rationale: Total = car daily price x rental days + additional services costs + different-city fee; computed by the backend so the frontend never calculates amounts, avoiding client-side pricing inconsistencies.

nodes.label: Bank Payment Processors (Ziraat Bank, Akbank)
nodes.rationale: System integrates multiple bank processors; the backend automatically selects the appropriate processor, keeping gateway choice invisible to the frontend.

nodes.label: RentalCar Entity
nodes.rationale: Rental transaction linking car, customer, pickup city and return city with start/finish/return dates and start/return kilometers.

nodes.label: Rental Process Flow
nodes.rationale: Prescribed order: check availability via getCarRentalStatus, create rental (addForIndividualCustomer or addForCorporateCustomer), add ordered additionals, then process payment. Date validation: start date today or future, finish after start, return date between start and finish.

nodes.label: Rental Status Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Statuses derived from dates rather than a stored flag: Active (started, returnDate null), Completed (returnDate set), Upcoming (start date in future).

nodes.label: User Entity
nodes.rationale: Base entity for all users (customers and administrators); emails unique system-wide; passwords stored with bcrypt hashing.

nodes.label: JWT Authentication Flow (Frontend Guide)
nodes.rationale: Register via /api/auth/register, login via /api/auth/login which returns a JWT; token sent as 'Authorization: Bearer {token}' on all authenticated requests; admin-only endpoints return 403 for regular users; users can only access their own data except admins.
